---
title: 'Rural employment trends by Census Region: West leads recovery'
description: Line chart showing rural employment change by Census region since the
  Great Recession.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-rural-region-emp-change-since-gr-lc/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Labor Market
  - Rural Development
  - Geography
tags:
  - QCEW
  - Employment
  - Rural
  - Regional
  - Census Regions
  - Line Chart
image: 3.2_rural_region_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: 'Rural employment trends by Census Region: West leads recovery'
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  dataSource: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
usesPackages:
  - cori-data-bds
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart breaks down rural employment recovery by Census region, revealing significant geographic variation in labor market performance since 2007.](./3.2_rural_region_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

This chart breaks down rural employment recovery by Census region, revealing significant geographic variation in labor market performance since 2007.

## Key Findings

- The West region leads rural employment recovery
- The Northeast and Midwest rural areas lag behind in employment recovery
- Regional disparities in rural employment have widened since the Great Recession

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/emp_change_lc.R` in the producing project.
